flagged.detail:
The Thymine cloze deletion swallows block-level
tags (including an empty
and an opening
closed only after the }}), so the cloze straddles HTML structure; it renders but the deletion markup is malformed.
The Khan Academy link in the Extra field has a stray " entity at the start of its href value, producing a broken URL; the card's question/answer text still renders fine.
Cloze deletion content is an image of the density equation, so the revealed answer adds no usable text beyond the prompt 'Give the equation for density'.
flagged.evidence: {{c1::}} -> answer_text: '...Give the equation for density : YouTube Link'

flagged.detail: Cloze deletion content is an image of the equation, so the revealed answer adds no usable text beyond the prompt 'Give the equation for magnetic force of a moving point charge'.
flagged.evidence: {{c1::}} -> answer_text ends '...moving point charge : Khan Academy Link'

flagged.detail: Cloze deletion content is an image of the equation, so the revealed answer adds no usable text beyond the prompt 'Give the equation for magnetic force of a current carrying wire'.
flagged.evidence: {{c1::}} -> answer_text ends '...current carrying wire : Khan Academy Link'

notes: Batch 03 is 257 MileDown cloze cards (Biochemistry/DNA-RNA, Behavioral neuroscience, and Physics fluids/electrostatics/magnetism/circuits) and is very clean: cloze {{cN::}} braces are balanced and well-formed, Q/A correspondence is accurate, and no LaTeX delimiters are used (equations are rendered as images). No errors were found. The few flags are low severity: three 'Give the equation' cards whose cloze deletion is an image so the plain-text answer reveals nothing (info), one cloze that awkwardly spans <div> tags (warning), and one Khan Academy link with a malformed href (info).
